_meta.purpose: Fish Audio S2.1 Pro voice mapping for the 24 dialogue archetypes (Phase A of the Fish Audio v2 revoice, branch overhaul/mothership-rewrite). Consumed by tools/audio_gen/fish_batch.py: archetypes[TAG].primary.voice_id is the reference_id sent to the API; archetypes[TAG].delivery_baseline is prepended to any line whose text has no leading [tag].
_meta.matching_criteria: group personality (CH/EV/GR/PA per docs/dialogue_spec.md), sex, and rank-implied age (captains/officers skew older). Source data: resources/dialogue/archetypes/*.json blurbs, speech_quirks, and elevenlabs_voice descriptions.

double_duty.rationale: 11 ML human archetypes vs 10 ML human voices forces exactly one voice to double up. These two are the least-conflicting pair available: same personality group (cheerful), same rank tier (officer), closest age band (early-to-mid 30s), and near-identical source voice descriptions ('warm ... tenor ... brisk/upbeat ... reassuring-cheerful'). Caveat: both are officers of different careers (engineering vs medical) and could plausibly crew the same ship at once. If that reads as a collision in playtesting, move CH_ML_SCI_OF to its own alternate (young tired, b605ba21a5994bc29c620e487b65884f) — CH_ML_MAR_CM already holds the pool's other young high-energy/friendly voice separately, so it is unaffected.

other_flagged_compromises: GR_ML_SCI_OF gets 'young gruff' instead of a middle-aged voice: both middle-aged-gruff slots went to the two GR_ML engineer archetypes (captain + crew mate). Age is a compromise; the dry/blunt gruff texture and 'flat matter-of-fact' delivery still land.

PA_ML_MAR_OF gets 'middle-aged tired': both young-paranoid slots went to the two PA_ML_SCI archetypes (captain + crew mate) since paranoia is their entire defining trait. No high-strung/forceful male voice remained in the pool, so this is the weakest primary match in the set — promote its 'young gruff' alternate if the smoke test reads too flat for a 'tight, forceful, wound-up' officer.
PA_ML_SCI_CA (captain) gets 'young paranoid' despite captains skewing older: no middle-aged-paranoid voice exists in the pool, and the paranoid personality match dominates the age mismatch.

PA_FE_MAR_CA gets 'young gruff' (female) rather than a paranoid voice: the pool's only young-paranoid female voice went to PA_FE_ENG_CM (exact age+personality match). The rasped/husky texture of the gruff voice reasonably covers her 'bright, forward, rasped alto ... husky laugh' description.

primary.rationale: Mid-20s bright energetic cheerful engineer ('whistles while she works') — young friendly is a direct personality+age match.

alternates.rationale: Twin friendly voice; swap if CH_FE_SCI_CM needs to trade with this one instead.
Fallback for a calmer take on her focused-puzzle-solving beats.

CH_FE_ENG_CM.delivery_baseline: [bright, upbeat, a little breathless with enthusiasm]

primary.rationale: Mid-20s bright buoyant medic fresh off residency; second young-friendly slot keeps her audibly distinct from the engineer.

alternates.rationale: Sibling friendly voice, usable if the two cheerful crew mates need to swap.
Fallback for her rare 'actually scared' flattened moments.

primary.rationale: Earnest try-hard cheerfulness with a comedic 'almost-but-not-quite human' edge — 'funny' captures the over-literal jokes and eager-to-please warmth.

alternates.rationale: Cleaner fallback if 'funny' overplays the comedy for serious lines.
For the under-stress beats where his cheerfulness just gets faster and more clipped, not more emotive.

primary.rationale: Warm mentor-energy officer, brisk and encouraging — closest personality/texture match in the pool. DOUBLE DUTY with CH_ML_SCI_OF, see _meta.double_duty.

alternates.rationale: The other high-energy slot, already primary for CH_ML_MAR_CM but usable in a pinch.
For extra officer gravitas if the double-duty pairing reads too similar to CH_ML_SCI_OF.

primary.rationale: Loud buddy-energy marine, late 20s — second high-energy slot, keeps him distinct from the engineering officer.

alternates.rationale: Sibling high-energy voice.
For the sharp military-cadence snap when things go bad mid-firefight.

primary.rationale: Warm reassuring-cheerful medic officer, early 30s. DOUBLE DUTY with CH_ML_ENG_OF — nearest voice in age/personality of any pair in the pool, see _meta.double_duty.

alternates.rationale: Use this instead of the double-duty voice if CH_ML_ENG_OF and CH_ML_SCI_OF need to be told apart.
Softer bedside-manner fallback; also the recommended swap target per the double-duty caveat.

primary.rationale: 'Composed and helpful... the uncanny edge shows only in how little the tone moves' — deadpan is a direct match.

alternates.rationale: Cleaner fallback with less deadpan flatness.
Least likely fit; only for scenes deliberately playing her uncanny precision for dry humor.

primary.rationale: Mid-30s composed, quietly authoritative marine officer; the pool has no dedicated 'even/steady' tag, so low-energy is the best proxy.

alternates.rationale: Sibling low-energy voice.
Slightly older read if she should sound more senior than the SCI officer of the same rank.

primary.rationale: Mid-40s captain with clinical detachment and a 'low controlled alto' — best age + even-as-low-energy + captain-gravitas match in the set.

alternates.rationale: Younger-sounding fallback.
Second young low-energy fallback.

primary.rationale: Late-30s 'warm but level' medic officer; second low-energy slot, kept in a younger age tier than the captain of the same career.

alternates.rationale: Sibling low-energy voice.
Older read if she should sound closer in age to EV_FE_SCI_CA.

primary.rationale: Early-40s steady engineer whose 'calm and procedural' delivery reads well against a low-energy/tired register.

alternates.rationale: Sibling tired voice.
Younger-sounding fallback.

primary.rationale: Early-30s marine whose entire personality is stillness; the younger tired voice keeps him distinct in age from the older engineer crew mate.

alternates.rationale: Older-sounding fallback.
Second middle-aged-tired fallback.

primary.rationale: 'Curmudgeonly-affectionate' grumbling has a dry comic undertone — funny captures the performed grumpiness of an android imitating old spacers.

alternates.rationale: Cleaner fallback.
For the moments the performance drops entirely into flat synthetic precision under real danger.

primary.rationale: Early-60s warm-worn gravelly officer — direct gruff+age+rank match.

alternates.rationale: Sibling gruff voice.
Fallback for her low-mutter coaxing-a-machine moments.

primary.rationale: Mid-40s low rough alto marine; second gruff slot, kept distinct from the engineering officer.

alternates.rationale: Sibling gruff voice.
Younger-sounding fallback for a harder-edged read.

primary.rationale: Mid-50s captain, 'grounded weathered baritone... calm authority earned rather than performed' — best gruff+age+rank match in the set.

alternates.rationale: Sibling gruff voice, already primary for GR_ML_ENG_CM.
Fallback if a younger-sounding captain is wanted.

primary.rationale: Late-60s flat gravelled crew mate; second gruff slot, the oldest-sounding texture available in the pool.

alternates.rationale: Sibling gruff voice, already primary for GR_ML_ENG_CA.
Fallback for his slow, half-second-pause delivery.

primary.rationale: Late-50s dry, blunt doctor. Both middle-aged-gruff slots went to the two GR_ML engineer archetypes, so this is an age compromise (flagged in _meta) — the dry/gruff texture and flat matter-of-fact delivery still land well.

alternates.rationale: Better age match if reassigned; would require re-homing GR_ML_ENG_CM.
Same trade-off as above.

primary.rationale: Vigilant, precise, 'coiled-tight' — a clean baseline voice for a character whose affect otherwise stays flat and controlled.

alternates.rationale: For her rare attempts at sounding casual, which 'don't quite land' — deadpan undercuts it nicely.
Least likely fit; only for a lighter take.

primary.rationale: Early-20s nervous jittery engineer — exact personality+age match, the pool's one dedicated young-paranoid female voice.

alternates.rationale: For her rare 'hands on the problem, mind quiet' focused beats.
Fallback with more edge if the low-energy take reads too calm.

primary.rationale: Early-to-mid-30s captain, 'bright, forward, rasped alto... quick husky laugh' — the rasp reads as gruff texture even though her personality group is paranoid, not gruff. The pool's only young-paranoid female voice went to the younger engineer crew mate, so this is a flagged compromise (see _meta).

alternates.rationale: Sibling gruff voice, slightly older-reading.
More command-voice weight if the smoke test wants her older-sounding.

primary.rationale: Mid-30s 'tight, forceful, wound-up' officer. Both young-paranoid slots went to the two PA_ML_SCI archetypes, so no high-strung male voice remained — texture-of-last-resort, flagged in _meta as the weakest primary match in the set.

alternates.rationale: More forceful edge; promote to primary if the smoke test reads too flat for this archetype.
Sibling tired voice.

primary.rationale: Late-30s captain, reedy anxious-analytical tenor. Captains skew older, but no middle-aged-paranoid voice exists in the pool and the paranoid personality match dominates the age gap (flagged in _meta).

alternates.rationale: Sibling paranoid voice, already primary for PA_ML_SCI_CM.
Older-sounding fallback for his rare moments of captain's-command flatness.

primary.rationale: Mid-20s hedging, nervous crew mate — exact age+personality match, the cleanest assignment in the whole set.

alternates.rationale: Sibling paranoid voice, already primary for PA_ML_SCI_CA.
Fallback for his sudden nervous-pause beats.

female_human.note: Held as PA_FE_MAR_CA's first alternate; unused as a primary.
No middle-aged-cheerful female archetype exists in the current 24 — both CH_FE archetypes are mid-20s crew mates.

android.note: Held as CH_ML_AND_CM's first alternate; unused as a primary.
Held as CH_ML_AND_CM's second alternate; unused as a primary. Only 1 of 4 android archetypes is male-coded, so 2 of the 3 male android voices are spare.
